full_name: Ministry of Public Safety and Solicitor General
blurb: Keeps British Columbians safe — policing oversight, corrections and community supervision, victim services, road safety, security industry licensing, civil forfeiture and death investigations. Note: liquor and cannabis licensing and enforcement moved to the Ministry of Agriculture and Food in July 2025, and gambling regulation and enforcement (formerly the Gaming Policy and Enforcement Branch) moved to the independent Gambling Control Office effective April 13, 2026 under the modernized Gaming Control Act.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians are protected by effective policing and community safety programs
goals.description: The ministry sets provincial policing standards, oversees the structure of police services, licenses the security industry, and runs crime-prevention programs including criminal record checks for people working with children and vulnerable adults.

main_services.name: Access policing services in your community

supporting.name: Find your local police service
supporting.desc: British Columbia receives policing from RCMP federal, provincial and municipal forces, a First Nations administered police service, and transit and railway police; this directory helps residents find who polices their area.

legislation.full_name: Police Act  -  Provision and structure of police services

supporting.name: Serve on a municipal police board
supporting.desc: Municipalities with populations of 5,000 or more are policed by an independent municipal police department or an RCMP municipal unit, and independent departments are governed by a municipal police board under the Police Act.

channels.label: The structure of police services in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Police Act  -  Municipal police boards

supporting.name: Volunteer with police through the Auxiliary Program
supporting.desc: The Auxiliary Program lets members of the public volunteer alongside police on patrols, community events, traffic control and crime-prevention activities under an officer's supervision.

channels.label: The structure of police services in B.C. (Auxiliary Program)

legislation.full_name: Police Act  -  Auxiliary constables

supporting.name: Prevent and respond to firearm and gang violence
supporting.desc: The Firearm Violence Prevention Act gives police new powers to remove firearms from high-risk situations, and provincial programs coordinate crime-reduction and organized crime prevention efforts.

legislation.full_name: Firearm Violence Prevention Act  -  Firearm violence prevention orders

supporting.name: Report extortion or organized crime activity
supporting.desc: The province works with police to investigate, prevent and stop extortion-related crimes targeting individuals and businesses in B.C. communities.
supporting.users: General public, businesses targeted by extortion

supporting.name: Get a security worker licence
supporting.desc: Anyone working as a security guard, private investigator, locksmith, security alarm installer, security consultant, armoured car guard or body armour salesperson needs a security worker licence from the Security Programs Division.
supporting.users: Security guards, private investigators, locksmiths, alarm installers

channels.label: Check if a worker is licensed

legislation.full_name: Security Services Act  -  Security worker licensing

supporting.name: Get a security business licence
supporting.desc: Companies that provide security guarding, alarm, investigation or related services need a security business licence from the Security Programs Division.

channels.label: Check if a business is licensed

legislation.full_name: Security Services Act  -  Security business licensing

relationships.service: Report a concern about a security worker or business

supporting.name: Report a concern about a security worker or business
supporting.desc: The public can submit a complaint about a licensed security worker or business, which the Security Programs Division investigates and enforces under the Security Services Act.
supporting.users: General public, clients of security businesses

legislation.full_name: Security Services Act  -  Compliance and enforcement

supporting.name: Get permits for armoured vehicles or body armour
supporting.desc: Businesses and individuals need provincial permits to operate an armoured vehicle or to possess body armour in B.C.
supporting.users: Armoured vehicle operators, body armour buyers

legislation.full_name: Security Services Act  -  Armoured vehicle and body armour permits

main_services.name: Get a criminal record check for work with children or vulnerable adults

supporting.name: Get a criminal record check through the Criminal Records Review Program
supporting.desc: People who work or volunteer with children under 19 or vulnerable adults must complete a vulnerable sector check through the Criminal Records Review Program, run by the Security Programs Division.

channels.label: Apply for a criminal record check

legislation.full_name: Criminal Records Review Act  -  Vulnerable sector criminal record checks

relationships.service: Get certified as an early childhood educator
Screen employees and volunteers as an organization

supporting.name: Screen employees and volunteers as an organization
supporting.desc: Organizations covered by the Criminal Records Review Act must screen people who work with children or vulnerable adults through the CRRP and manage screening through an organization account.

legislation.full_name: Criminal Records Review Act  -  Organization screening duties

relationships.service: Get a criminal record check through the Criminal Records Review Program
Open a licensed child care facility

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: People affected by crime get support, and offenders are safely supervised
goals.description: The ministry funds victim services and financial assistance for people affected by crime, maintains the province-wide protection order registry, and BC Corrections manages adults in custody and under community supervision.

main_services.name: Get support as a victim of crime

supporting.name: Get 24/7 crisis support and referrals as a victim of crime
supporting.desc: VictimLinkBC is a toll-free, confidential, multilingual phone and text service available 24/7 across B.C. and the Yukon, providing information, referrals and immediate crisis support to victims of crime, family and sexual violence.
supporting.users: Victims of crime, witnesses, family members

legislation.full_name: Victims of Crime Act  -  Victim services

supporting.name: Apply for crime victim assistance benefits
supporting.desc: The Crime Victim Assistance Program provides medical, counselling, income-support, funeral and other benefits to victims of violent crime, immediate family members and some witnesses in B.C.
supporting.users: Victims of violent crime, immediate family members, witnesses

channels.label: Crime Victim Assistance Program application forms

legislation.full_name: Crime Victim Assistance Act  -  Benefits for victims, family members and witnesses

supporting.name: Register for notification about an offender's status
supporting.desc: The Victim Safety Unit lets registered victims receive ongoing information about the custody status, release dates and conditions of an accused or convicted offender.

legislation.full_name: Victims of Crime Act  -  Victim notification

supporting.name: Search or register a protection order
supporting.desc: The Protection Order Registry is a confidential, province-wide database of civil and criminal protection orders that lets police confirm and enforce orders any time, day or night.
supporting.users: Survivors of family or intimate partner violence, police

legislation.full_name: Police Act  -  Protection Order Registry

supporting.name: Get help travelling to a justice-related proceeding
supporting.desc: The Victim Travel Fund provides funding for victims or family members to attend justice-related proceedings elsewhere in B.C.

main_services.name: Serve or manage a custodial or community sentence

supporting.name: Find a correctional centre or probation office
supporting.desc: BC Corrections operates correctional centres for adults in custody and probation offices that supervise people serving community sentences such as bail or probation orders.
supporting.users: People in custody, people on probation, families

legislation.full_name: Correction Act  -  Custody and community supervision

supporting.name: Access programs to reduce reoffending while in custody or on probation
supporting.desc: BC Corrections offers programs addressing substance use, violence prevention, intimate partner violence and life and work skills to help people in custody or under supervision reduce reoffending.
supporting.users: People in custody, people on community supervision

legislation.full_name: Correction Act  -  Correctional programs

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Unlawful proceeds are seized and unexplained deaths are independently investigated
goals.description: The Civil Forfeiture Office targets the proceeds and instruments of unlawful activity, and the BC Coroners Service independently investigates unnatural, sudden, unexpected, unexplained or unattended deaths to improve public safety.

main_services.name: Report and recover proceeds of unlawful activity

supporting.name: Report suspected proceeds or instruments of unlawful activity
supporting.desc: The Civil Forfeiture Office targets property that is the proceeds or an instrument of unlawful activity so people cannot profit from crime or use property to seriously harm others.

legislation.full_name: Civil Forfeiture Act  -  Forfeiture of unlawfully obtained property

supporting.name: Apply for a crime-prevention grant or victim compensation from forfeited funds
supporting.desc: The Director of Civil Forfeiture disburses forfeited funds to community crime-prevention grants and to compensate eligible victims of unlawful activity.
supporting.users: Community organizations, victims of unlawful activity

legislation.full_name: Civil Forfeiture Act  -  Disbursement of forfeited funds

main_services.name: Get information about a death investigated by the coroner

supporting.name: Report a death to the BC Coroners Service
supporting.desc: Anyone can report an unnatural, sudden, unexpected, unexplained or unattended death to the BC Coroners Service, which investigates and classifies the death and may make recommendations to prevent future deaths.

legislation.full_name: Coroners Act  -  Duty to report and investigate reportable deaths

supporting.name: Request a coroner's report or family resources
supporting.desc: Families and other eligible parties can request a coroner's investigation or inquest report, and access family resources on coping with bereavement and loss.
supporting.users: Families of the deceased, funeral homes

legislation.full_name: Coroners Act  -  Coroner's reports and inquests

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Roads are safer and fire risks are managed through licensing, standards and enforcement
goals.description: RoadSafetyBC assesses driver medical fitness, manages high-risk driving prohibitions and vehicle impoundment to work toward zero traffic deaths, and the Office of the Fire Commissioner enforces B.C.'s fire safety legislation and training standards.

main_services.name: Keep your driver's licence and driving privileges in good standing

supporting.name: Get your driver's medical fitness assessed
supporting.desc: RoadSafetyBC assesses drivers' medical fitness to drive and makes licensing decisions, including reviewing driver medical examination reports and requests to review a fitness decision.
supporting.users: Drivers with a medical condition, health professionals

legislation.full_name: Motor Vehicle Act  -  Driver medical fitness and Superintendent of Motor Vehicles

supporting.name: Apply for a review of a driving prohibition or suspension
supporting.desc: Drivers can apply online for a review of alcohol- or drug-related driving prohibitions and suspensions, including 24-hour, 3-, 7-, 30- and 90-day prohibitions issued under the Motor Vehicle Act.
supporting.users: Drivers who have received a prohibition or suspension

channels.label: Apply for a driving prohibition review

legislation.full_name: Motor Vehicle Act  -  Driving prohibitions and reviews

supporting.name: Get your vehicle back after an impoundment
supporting.desc: The Vehicle Impoundment Program explains how to recover a vehicle impounded for high-risk driving offences, and the fees and conditions that apply.

legislation.full_name: Motor Vehicle Act  -  Vehicle impoundment

supporting.name: Complete a high-risk driver program
supporting.desc: The Driver Improvement Program, Ignition Interlock Program and Responsible Driver Program help high-risk drivers address unsafe driving behaviour and regain driving privileges.

legislation.full_name: Motor Vehicle Act  -  High-risk driver programs

supporting.name: Report a fire incident
supporting.desc: Building owners, fire departments and other parties are required to report specific fires to the Office of the Fire Commissioner under B.C.'s fire safety legislation.

legislation.full_name: Fire Safety Act  -  Mandatory fire incident reporting

supporting.name: Get trained and certified as a firefighter, fire inspector or investigator
supporting.desc: The Office of the Fire Commissioner sets and delivers minimum training standards for firefighters, fire inspectors and fire investigators across B.C.

legislation.full_name: Fire Safety Act  -  Training standards for fire service personnel

supporting.name: Apply for a fire service award
supporting.desc: Firefighters and citizens who perform exemplary fire-related service may be eligible for a fire service award or recognition through the Office of the Fire Commissioner.

foundational_services.name: Provincial policing standards and oversight policy
foundational_services.desc: Policy and standard-setting work for provincial policing standards, the Police Tax financing model, and coordination of federal, provincial, municipal, First Nations and integrated police services.

legislation.full_name: Police Act  -  Provincial policing standards

foundational_services.name: Security Programs Division licence compliance and risk assessment
foundational_services.desc: Back-office licensing administration, risk assessment, fingerprinting coordination and compliance monitoring for the security industry and criminal record check programs.

foundational_services.name: Victim services program funding and coordination
foundational_services.desc: Funding, coordination and policy support for community-based victim services and violence-against-women programs across British Columbia.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, victim service providers

foundational_services.name: BC Corrections research, evaluation and reporting
foundational_services.desc: Research, program evaluation and public reporting on the adult correctional population, reoffending outcomes and the Indigenous Justice Framework.

mandate_minister: Nina Krieger (Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General); Terry Yung (Minister of State for Community Safety and Integrated Services)
